inlineContent.text: YEOFR recognises an enrolled user from the front camera and rejects everyone
else, fusing three signals into one trust decision: face recognition, a
liveness CNN, and TrueDepth. The recognised path is
the engine, the camera, the fused-trust pipeline, the identity gate, and
tracker persistence, and exposes two streams:

the in-flight Vision flag, last-run timing, and the rolling

The specific framing problem that made a face
invalid, such as no face, too close, or too far.
Progress for an in-flight enrolment capture. Yielded by
and the stream finishes once the
gallery is banked (or the capture can’t start).
Why a liveness check could not reach a live-or-spoof verdict — for
example still initialising or insufficient depth data this frame.
A concrete, value-type
you can build and
hand to the trust pipeline to enable or disable individual signals.
Manages the front-camera
and feeds frames — plus
TrueDepth, on supported devices — into the trust pipeline.
Result of a single
invocation. The detection half
is the synchronous FR pass for this frame; the evaluation half is
the
trust verdict (which may incorporate prior frames’
liveness results — the CNN runs asynchronously, so this frame’s
CNN verdict typically lands ~1 frame later).
Subscribe to the rolling trust verdict stream. The current
is yielded immediately on subscribe;
subsequent values fire whenever observable state changes
(trust, depth, liveness, face counts, anti-spoof signals).
Recognise the enrolled user and reject everyone else, in a few lines.
Public per-frame fusion trace for debug UI / CSV capture.
Mirrors the inputs that drove
plus the
hysteresis state, so a host can reconstruct exactly why the
emitted
differs from the raw fused decision.
The recognition engine’s lifecycle state — for example warming up,
ready, or in error — surfaced so the UI can gate capture until it is ready.
Neutral namespace for the public enrolment value types. (Caseless
enum — never instantiated; it only carries the nested types whose
fully-qualified names are part of the SDK’s public surface.)
Project version number for YEOFR.
Diagnostic snapshot of the recognition latch’s internal state for
one frame. Pushed by
so the per-frame trace can
surface
the latch is (or isn’t) admitting — the cold enter
floor, whether it has been lowered, and the rolling enter-hit count.
Diagnostic-only: it never changes the emitted trust verdict.
On-device face recognition with fused liveness and TrueDepth anti-spoofing.
The quantitative detail behind a
: the model’s
confidence and the measured face distance in metres.
Per-frame trust verdicts for the recognition UI. Call once and iterate;
the stream finishes on
. Newest-wins (the underlying camera
stream drops stale frames), so a slow consumer can’t back up memory.
A normalised status code returned by the recognition engine for an
operation such as detection, enrolment, or tracker loading.
One per-frame trust readout, with the framing gate already applied — so the
consumer renders a state, not a fusion of raw signals. Yielded by
Run one frame through the fusion pipeline. The returned
is sync and authoritative for this frame; the
returned
is the latest trust state (it may not
include this frame’s CNN verdict yet since the CNN runs
off-actor on a detached task).
Target head orientation for one slot of a gallery enrollment.
Which rule admitted (or denied) trust for a frame, so a log reviewer
sees the SDK’s ground-truth decision path. Records the
reason; the trailing
AND-gate can still drive
the final
to false (logged separately via the FR-match column).
Authenticated-encryption protocol the SDK funnels all sensitive
material through. Implementations must be safe to share across
threads (
) and must produce blobs whose
matches the byte returned by
The toggles the FR-trust pipeline reads to decide which signals — the
liveness CNN and TrueDepth — contribute to a frame’s trust verdict.
Selects how biometric payloads are encrypted before they are written to
disk —
(no encryption) or a consumer-supplied
cryptor.
Results for one frame
Snapshot of every signal-fed value plus the accumulator’s
derived trust verdict for one frame. Broadcast via
after every
that changes
observable state.
Versioned envelope around a cryptor’s payload. The
representation is what gets persisted or sent over the wire; the

(with a
explaining the depth-geometry failure), or

Generates and persists a symmetric encryption key in the Keychain,
creating one on first use and returning the same key on every call after.
Termination policy for the merged lighting phase — how long the
trailing-front “find new light” hunt runs and what ends it.
Project version string for YEOFR.
Forward a TrueDepth verdict from the host’s depth pipeline
into the evaluator. The session owns no camera or depth
hardware — depth wiring stays in the host (per the SDK
boundary), but the per-frame trust decision needs the verdict to
drive the depth-primary gate.
Enrol
from the live camera and return a stream of capture
progress. The existing frame loop drives the gallery walk; on completion
the service persists the gallery, refreshes
, resets the
trust pipeline against the fresh gallery, and re-arms the identity gate —
the consumer does none of that.
Why a frame is not evaluable. Carried on
so
consumers can render distinct UX for “no face”, “too close”, etc.,
instead of treating every non-evaluable frame as a spoof.
Typed failures surfaced to callers. Wrap any underlying CryptoKit
/ CommonCrypto error so the public surface stays one error type.
